title: OpenStack Galera / MariaDB Cluster Non-Primary or Split-Brain Indicators
app: Universal Forwarder on database nodes, custom `wsrep_status` scripted input
spl:
index=openstack (sourcetype="mysql:galera" OR sourcetype=syslog) earliest=-30m
| search wsrep OR "Galera" OR "non-Primary" OR "Non-primary"
| eval cluster_ok=coalesce(wsrep_cluster_status, cluster_status)
| where cluster_ok!="Primary" OR like(lower(_raw), "%non-primary%") OR like(lower(_raw), "%partition%")
| stats latest(cluster_ok) as status, values(wsrep_local_state_comment) as state by host
| sort status
description: The OpenStack SQL database underpins Keystone, Nova, Neutron, and Cinder metadata. A Galera node that is not `Primary` or shows partition comments risks write outages and API 500s across the cloud.
value: Stops multi-service OpenStack brownouts by detecting Galera cluster role loss before API error storms peak.
implementation: Run `mysql -e "SHOW STATUS LIKE 'wsrep%';"` on a schedule and HEC JSON, or parse error log lines. Tag events with `openstack_db` index. Alert immediately when any node reports non-Primary. Require two-source confirmation before automation to avoid flapping.
visualization: Table (node cluster state), Single value (non-primary count), Timeline (wsrep transitions).
